reasoning: The Lean signature encodes each element of the informal Baire theorem exactly: `[MetricSpace X] [CompleteSpace X] [Nonempty X]` matches "nonempty complete metric space"; `G : ℕ → Set X` with `∀ n, IsOpen (G n) ∧ Dense (G n)` matches "sequence of dense open sets {G_n}"; and the conclusion `∃ x, ∀ n, x ∈ G n` is logically equivalent to `(⋂ n, G n).Nonempty`, which is the claim that the countable intersection is not empty. No hypotheses are added or weakened, the quantifier structure is preserved, and no stricter/looser topological notions are substituted. Hence the formalization is strictly faithful.
